title: Numerical Input Closure Map Theorem

formulas.H_nu_0: [[lambda0+Delta0, etaA exp(i phiA), etaB exp(i phiB)], [etaA exp(-i phiA), lambda3+DeltaA, delta exp(i varphi)], [etaB exp(-i phiB), delta exp(-i varphi), lambda3+DeltaB]]
formulas.sector_displacement: Delta y_ud = - H_topo^{-1} grad_y(delta S_partial^d - delta S_partial^u)|_{y0}
formulas.S_nu_topo_gaussian_hessian: S_nu_topo = 1/2 Delta y_nu^T G_nu_topo Delta y_nu + S_barrier
formulas.S_eff_nu: S_eff^(nu) = S_bulk[Phi] + S_partial^(nu)[Phi] + S_subsurface^(nu)[Phi; ellapse_nu, g_sub]
formulas.S_eff_nu_boundary: S_eff^(nu) = S_bulk[Phi] + int_partialB [1/2 chi_nu^{AB} partial_A Phi partial_B Phi + lambda_nu(nhat) Phi nhat.grad Phi] dA
formulas.S_eff_nu_profile: S_eff^(nu)[W_nu] = int_B W_nu(y) V_eff^(nu)(y) dV + regularization/width term
formulas.subsurface_metric_split: ds_ext^2 = g_ext,ab dx^a dx^b; ds_sub^2 = g_sub,AB dY^A dY^B
formulas.ellapse_nu_topographic: 0 < ellapse_nu(y) <= 1 with ellapse_nu(y) -> 0 near a zero-exterior-spacetime core
formulas.neutral_projection_saddle: grad_Y S_eff^(nu)(Y_nu) = 0; y_nu = Pi_sub_to_ext(Y_nu); Delta y_nu = Pi_sub_to_ext(Y_nu) - y_H
formulas.exterior_projected_apparent_speed: v_ext,proj = |d Pi_sub_to_ext(Y) / dt_ext| with dt_ext = ellapse_nu dt_sub

topographic_suppression_action.name: Neutral topographic suppression action
topographic_suppression_action.formula_candidate: S_nu_topo = 1/2 Delta y_nu^T G_nu_topo Delta y_nu + S_barrier

candidate_routes.formula: S_nu_topo = 1/2 Delta y_nu^T G_nu_topo Delta y_nu + S_barrier
candidate_routes.reason: The repo has boundary Hessian/topographic scaffolds, but not a derived neutral displacement, neutral Hessian, embedding tensor, or barrier value.
candidate_routes.allowed_next_step: derive Delta y_nu, H_topo^(nu), E_nu, and S_barrier from the boundary/topographic action before comparison
candidate_routes.forbidden_shortcut: fit S_nu_topo or epsilon_nu_topo to observed neutrino masses

candidate_routes.reason: A stiffness/action mismatch is compatible with the topographic picture, but the neutral and charged effective saddles are not computed in the repo.
candidate_routes.allowed_next_step: construct S_eff and evaluate it on internally derived neutral and charged saddles
candidate_routes.forbidden_shortcut: fit or choose a stiffness mismatch from neutrino scale residuals

candidate_routes.reason: Omega_nu localizes a neutral sector operator and k-barrier hint, but no positive action mapping from Omega_nu to S_nu_topo is derived.
candidate_routes.allowed_next_step: derive a positive neutral barrier functional from Omega_nu inside the boundary action
candidate_routes.forbidden_shortcut: turn k values into a fitted suppression exponent

topographic_suppression_action.claim_boundary: The neutral topographic suppression action is localized as the next numerical-closure object. A candidate Hessian/barrier formula is identified, but numerical neutrino closure remains open unless explicitly derived and locked before comparison.
topographic_suppression_action.next_action: derive or reject Delta y_nu, H_topo^(nu), E_nu, and S_barrier from the scalar/topographic boundary action before any neutrino-scale comparison

subsurface_projection_geometry.name: Subsurface neutral projection geometry
subsurface_projection_geometry.formula_candidate: ds_sub^2 = g_sub,AB dY^A dY^B; x^a = Pi_sub_to_ext^a(Y); ellapse_nu^2 = ds_proj^2 / ds_sub^2
subsurface_projection_geometry.derivation_source: candidate internal/subsurface topographic metric split and exterior projection/lapse scaffold

candidate_routes.formula: ds_ext^2 = g_ext,ab dx^a dx^b; ds_sub^2 = g_sub,AB dY^A dY^B; x^a = Pi_sub_to_ext^a(Y); ellapse_nu^2 = ds_proj^2 / ds_sub^2
candidate_routes.reason: The metric split localizes the projection geometry, but the subsurface metric and projection map are not derived from the boundary/topographic action.
candidate_routes.allowed_next_action: derive g_sub and Pi_sub_to_ext from the internal topographic metric and boundary profile before comparison
candidate_routes.forbidden_shortcut: choose a metric ratio or projection map from neutrino residuals or anomalous propagation claims

candidate_routes.formula: 0 < ellapse_nu(y) <= 1 with ellapse_nu(y) -> 0 near a zero-exterior-spacetime core
candidate_routes.reason: A topographic lapse is structurally compatible with the subsurface-channel picture, but the scalar profile and boundary conditions are still open.
candidate_routes.allowed_next_action: derive ellapse_nu(y) from the scalar/topographic profile and boundary conditions
candidate_routes.forbidden_shortcut: fit ellapse_nu to neutrino masses, PMNS residuals, or apparent speed claims

candidate_routes.formula: grad_Y S_eff^(nu)(Y_nu) = 0; y_nu = Pi_sub_to_ext(Y_nu); Delta y_nu = Pi_sub_to_ext(Y_nu) - y_H
candidate_routes.reason: This route links projection geometry to the localized neutral saddle displacement, but S_eff_nu and Pi_sub_to_ext remain open.
candidate_routes.allowed_next_action: derive the neutral stationary channel and projection map before any neutrino comparison
candidate_routes.forbidden_shortcut: pick Y_nu or Pi_sub_to_ext to make Delta_y_nu numerically useful

candidate_routes.formula: v_sub <= c_sub; v_ext,proj = |d Pi_sub_to_ext(Y) / dt_ext|; dt_ext = ellapse_nu dt_sub
candidate_routes.reason: This explains how exterior-projected apparent FTL could be interpretive without local internal causality violation, but it is not derived until the metric, lapse, and projection map are derived.
candidate_routes.allowed_next_action: prove the projection statement from g_sub, ellapse_nu, and Pi_sub_to_ext
candidate_routes.forbidden_shortcut: claim literal local FTL, experimental FTL, or tune projection data to anomalies

subsurface_projection_geometry.claim_boundary: The subsurface neutral projection geometry has been localized as a required dependency for the neutral effective action. Candidate internal metric, projection map, and exterior-lapse structures are documented. No local FTL or numerical neutrino prediction is claimed.
subsurface_projection_geometry.causality_boundary: BHSM does not claim local causality violation. Any FTL-like behavior is exterior-projected apparent behavior arising from a subsurface/internal topographic channel and remains conditional until g_sub, ellapse_nu, and Pi_sub_to_ext are derived.
subsurface_projection_geometry.next_action: derive or reject g_sub, ellapse_nu, and Pi_sub_to_ext from the scalar/topographic profile, coordinate-invariant projection convention, positivity/causality proof, and neutral boundary tensors before comparison

neutral_effective_action.name: Neutral effective action
neutral_effective_action.formula_candidate: S_eff^(nu) = S_bulk[Phi] + S_partial^(nu)[Phi] + S_subsurface^(nu)[Phi; ellapse_nu, g_sub]
neutral_effective_action.derivation_source: candidate subsurface neutral topographic channel and boundary/profile action scaffolds

candidate_routes.formula: S_eff^(nu) = S_bulk[Phi] + S_partial^(nu)[Phi] + S_subsurface^(nu)[Phi; ellapse_nu, g_sub]
candidate_routes.reason: This route localizes the neutral effective action as a subsurface neutral topographic channel, but the internal/subsurface metric and projection/lapse map are not derived.
candidate_routes.allowed_next_step: derive g_sub, ellapse_nu, and the projection map from the internal topographic/boundary action before comparison
candidate_routes.forbidden_shortcut: fit a neutral effective action or apparent propagation parameter to neutrino data

candidate_routes.formula: S_eff^(nu) = S_bulk[Phi] + int_partialB [1/2 chi_nu^{AB} partial_A Phi partial_B Phi + lambda_nu(nhat) Phi nhat.grad Phi] dA
candidate_routes.reason: Scalar/topographic boundary terms give a plausible action source, but the neutral boundary tensor, normal coupling, and boundary condition remain open.
candidate_routes.allowed_next_step: derive chi_nu, lambda_nu, and the neutral boundary condition from the scalar/topographic boundary action
candidate_routes.forbidden_shortcut: choose boundary tensors to reproduce observed neutrino masses, splittings, or PMNS angles

candidate_routes.reason: Omega_nu is a sector label and boundary-operator hint, but the repo does not derive an action functional directly from Omega_nu.
candidate_routes.allowed_next_step: derive an operator-to-action map from the neutral boundary functional
candidate_routes.forbidden_shortcut: convert Omega_nu labels into a fitted neutral action scale

candidate_routes.formula: S_eff^(nu)[W_nu] = int_B W_nu(y) V_eff^(nu)(y) dV + regularization/width term
candidate_routes.reason: A finite-width neutral profile can define a saddle or centroid action, but the weight, potential, and regularization are not derived.
candidate_routes.allowed_next_step: derive W_nu, V_eff^(nu), and the regularization term before comparison
candidate_routes.forbidden_shortcut: select a neutral profile width to obtain a desired neutrino suppression

neutral_effective_action.claim_boundary: The neutral effective action S_eff^(nu) is localized as the action-level source for the neutral saddle displacement. The subsurface neutral-channel candidate is documented, but the internal metric, projection/lapse map, neutral boundary tensors, and neutral profile remain open. Numerical neutrino closure remains open.
neutral_effective_action.causality_boundary: Subsurface neutral topographic channel language permits exterior-projected anomalous propagation or apparent FTL from the exterior-surface viewpoint only as an interpretive projection; the candidate remains locally causal in the internal/topographic metric and does not assert locally acausal propagation.
neutral_effective_action.next_action: derive or reject g_sub, ellapse_nu, Pi_sub_to_ext, chi_nu, lambda_nu, W_nu, and V_eff^(nu) from the boundary/topographic action before any neutrino comparison

neutral_saddle_displacement.name: Neutral saddle displacement
neutral_saddle_displacement.derivation_source: candidate scalar/topographic stationary-point and finite-width centroid scaffolds

candidate_routes.formula: grad_y S_eff^(nu)(y_nu)=0; grad_y S_eff^(H)(y_H)=0; Delta y_nu = - H_H^{-1} grad_y[delta S_eff^(nu-H)]|_{y_H}
candidate_routes.reason: The repo has scalar/topographic and Hessian scaffolds, but not explicit neutral/Higgs effective actions or a derived linearized gradient.
candidate_routes.allowed_next_step: derive S_eff^(nu), S_eff^(H), H_H, and the neutral-minus-Higgs gradient from the boundary/topographic action before comparison
candidate_routes.forbidden_shortcut: fit Delta y_nu to observed neutrino masses or PMNS residuals

candidate_routes.reason: The neutral ledger is present, but the Higgs/charged reference label and label-to-coordinate displacement map are not derived.
candidate_routes.allowed_next_step: derive the Higgs/charged reference label and label-to-distance map from the boundary tensors
candidate_routes.forbidden_shortcut: choose a label displacement because it gives the desired neutrino suppression

candidate_routes.reason: Omega_nu identifies the neutral boundary operator, but no operator-to-displacement theorem is present.
candidate_routes.allowed_next_step: derive a map from boundary-operator mismatch to topographic displacement
candidate_routes.forbidden_shortcut: turn Omega_nu values into fitted displacement coordinates

candidate_routes.formula: y_nu = integral y W_nu(y) dV / integral W_nu(y) dV; y_H = integral y W_H(y) dV / integral W_H(y) dV; Delta y_nu = y_nu - y_H
candidate_routes.reason: The centroid definition is precise, but the neutral/Higgs weights and invariant centroid convention are not derived.
candidate_routes.allowed_next_step: derive W_nu, W_H, a coordinate chart, and an invariant centroid prescription from the finite-width profile
candidate_routes.forbidden_shortcut: choose centroids after looking at neutrino data

neutral_saddle_displacement.claim_boundary: The neutral saddle displacement Delta y_nu has been localized as a required input for the S_nu_topo Hessian/barrier formula. Candidate stationary-point and finite-width centroid definitions are documented. Numerical neutrino closure remains open.
neutral_saddle_displacement.next_action: derive or reject the stationary-point displacement ingredients or the finite-width centroid weights before any neutrino-scale comparison
